mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through increased corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC exceeding 0.05 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 55% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes > 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero rank IC for 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profitability, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive relationship with 20-day forward returns over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply-side weakness, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 58% of instances over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.
